concepts.title: Allotropy of Sulphur
concepts.conceptBody:
Allotropy and Polymorphism of Sulphur

Many different molecular species are possible for the elemental sulphur, and this accounts for the existence of the large number of physical forms in which the element may appear. Allotropy is more complex for the element sulphur than for any other element of group 16 (VI). Sulphur may exist as:

  • rhombic sulphur $\left( {{\mathbf{S}}_{\alpha }} \right)$ which has sixteen ${{\text{S}}_{8}}$ rings in a unit cell
  • monoclinic sulphur $\left( {{\mathbf{S}}_{\beta }} \right)$ which has six ${{\text{S}}_{8}}$ rings in its unit cell.
  • liquid sulphur $\left( {{\mathbf{S}}_{\mathbf{\lambda }}} \right)$ comprised of ${{\text{S}}_{8}}$ molecules. This is yellow, transparent, mobile liquid. At $160\text{C}$, a remarkable transformation occurs. The ${{\text{S}}_{8}}$ rings open up and join together into long spiral-chain molecules, resulting in:
  • liquid sulphur $\left( {{\mathbf{S}}_{\mu }} \right)$ which is dark in colour and very thick and viscous. The chain length and viscosity of the liquid reach a maximum at about $180\text{C}$. At higher temperatures the chains break up and viscosity decreases again. At $445\text{C}$, this liquid boils, producing:
  • sulphur vapours , which consists of molecules ranging from ${{\text{S}}_{2}}$ to ${{\text{S}}_{10}}$, but predominantly ${{\text{S}}_{8}}$. At higher temperatures, ${{\text{S}}_{2}}$ predominates. ${{\text{S}}_{2}}$ is paramagnetic.
  • Plastic sulphur forms if liquid sulphur $(\mu )$ is poured into cold water. It consists of chainlike molecules and has rubber-like properties when first formed. On standing, it becomes brittle and finally converts to rhombic sulphur. Above transformations can be written as:

\[{{\text{S}}_{\alpha }}\xrightarrow{95.5{}^\circ C}{{\text{S}}_{\beta }}\xrightarrow{160{}^\circ C}{{\text{S}}_{\mu }}\xrightarrow{445{}^\circ C}{{\text{S}}_{8}}(~\text{g})\]

$\text{S}\xrightarrow{2000{}^\circ C}{{\text{S}}_{2}}\xrightarrow{1000{}^\circ C}{{\text{S}}_{4}}\xleftarrow{{}}{{\text{S}}_{6}}$

  • If rhombic sulphur is heated rapidly, it fails to convert into monoclinic sulphur and melts at$113\text{C}$.
  • In cyclo-${{S}_{6}}$, the the ring adopts the chair form chain polymers, catena-${{\text{S}}_{\text{n}}}$ are also known .

concepts.title: Oxides of Sulphur
concepts.conceptBody:
Compound of Sulphur

Oxide: 

Sulphur forms several oxides of which sulphur dioxide $\left( \text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}} \right)$ and sulphur trioxide $\left( \text{S}{{\text{O}}_{3}} \right)$ are important.

Sulphur dioxide 

Preparation: 

$4\text{Fe}{{\text{S}}_{2}}+11{{\text{O}}_{2}}\to 2\text{F}{{\text{e}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+8\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$

Properties: 

1. As reducing agent

(i) Action on halogens:$\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+\text{C}{{\text{l}}_{2}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\xrightarrow{{}}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+2\text{HCl}$

(ii) Action on$\text{FeC}{{\text{l}}_{3}}$

$2\text{FeC}{{\text{l}}_{3}}+\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\xrightarrow{{}}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+2\text{FeC}{{\text{l}}_{2}}+2\text{HCl}$

2. Reaction with acidified $\text{KMn}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$

$2\text{KMn}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+5\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\xrightarrow{{}}{{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+2\text{MnS}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$

3. Reaction with acidified ${{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{r}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{7}}$

${{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{r}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{7}}+3\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}\xrightarrow{{}}{{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+\text{C}{{\text{r}}_{2}}{{\left( \text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}} \right)}_{3}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

4. Oxidising property

(i) $2{{\text{H}}_{2}}~\text{S}+\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}\xrightarrow{{}}2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}+\text{S}\downarrow $

(ii) $\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+2\text{Mg}\xrightarrow{{}}2\text{MgO}+\text{S}\downarrow $

Sulphur trioxide

Preparation: 

By dehydration of ${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$

${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}\xrightarrow[heat]{{{P}_{2}}{{O}_{5}}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

Reaction: ${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\xrightarrow{{}}{{\text{H}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{7}}$

${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$ saturated with $\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$ is called oleum.

concepts.title: Oxyacids of Sulphur
concepts.conceptBody:
Oxyacids of sulphur:

Based on the structural similarities, oxoacids or (oxyacids) of sulphur may be classified as:

1. Sulphoxylic acid, ${{\mathbf{H}}_{\mathbf{2}}}\mathbf{S}{{\mathbf{O}}_{\mathbf{2}}}$ 

2. Sulphurous acid series 

(a) Sulphurous acid, ${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$

(b) Thiosulphurous acid, ${{\text{H}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$

(c) Dithionus acid, ${{\text{H}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$

(d) Di or pyrosulphurous acid ${{\text{H}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{5}}$

3. Sulphuric acid series 

(a) Sulphuric acid, ${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$

(b) Thiosulphuric acid, ${{\text{H}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$

(c) Di or pyrosulphuric acid, ${{\text{H}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{7}}$

4. Peroxo-acid series 

Peroxomonosulphuric acid (also called Caro's acid, ${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{5}}$ )

Perdisulphuric acid (also called Marshall's acid, ${{\text{H}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{8}}$ )

(Structure of oxoacids of sulphur)

concepts.title: Sulphuric acid
concepts.conceptBody:
Sulphuric Acid $\left( {{\mathbf{H}}_{\mathbf{2}}}\mathbf{S}{{\mathbf{O}}_{\mathbf{4}}} \right)$

  • As an oxidizing agent

$2\text{HBr}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{B}{{\text{r}}_{2}}+\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+2{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

${{\text{K}}_{4}}\left[ \text{Fe}{{(\text{CN})}_{6}} \right]+6{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+6{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\xrightarrow{{}}2~{{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+\text{FeS}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+3{{\left( \text{N}{{\text{H}}_{4}} \right)}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+6\text{CO}$

$8\text{HI}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}\xrightarrow{{}}{{\text{H}}_{2}}~\text{S}+4{{\text{I}}_{2}}+4{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

  • As a dehydrating agent

$\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{OH}\xrightarrow{Conc.\,\,{{H}_{2}}S{{O}_{4}}}\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}=\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

$\text{HO}-\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}-\text{OH}+2\text{PC}{{\text{l}}_{5}}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{l}}_{2}}+2\text{POC}{{\text{l}}_{3}}+2\text{HCl}$

Sulphuryl Chloride

concepts.title: Sodium Thiosulphates
concepts.conceptBody:
Sodium thiosulphate $\left( \mathbf{N}{{\mathbf{a}}_{\mathbf{2}}}\mathbf{~}{{\mathbf{S}}_{\mathbf{2}}}{{\mathbf{O}}_{\mathbf{3}}}\mathbf{\times 5}{{\mathbf{H}}_{\mathbf{2}}}\mathbf{O} \right)$:

If one of the oxygen atoms in the sulplhate ion is replaced by sulphur, the resulting ion $\left( {{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}^{2-} \right)$ is known as thiosulphate.

Preparation: 

(i) Sodium thiosulphate is prepared by boiling aq. solution of metal sulplhites with elemental sulphur.

$\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+\frac{1}{8}~{{\text{S}}_{8}}\xrightarrow[{{H}_{2}}O]{373\,K}\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}$

Hydrated sodium thioosulphate $\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\cdot 5{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$ is known as HYPO.

(ii) Spring's reaction may be used for the preparation of sodium thiosulphate. It consists in treating a mixture of sodium sulphide and sodium sulphite with calculate quantity of iodine.

$\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~\text{S}+\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+{{\text{I}}_{2}}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+2\text{NaI}$

Properties: 

(i) Reaction with dilute acids: It reacts with dilute acids to liberate sulphur dioxide gas along with precipitate of sulphur.

$\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+2\text{HCl}\xrightarrow{{}}2\text{NaCl}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}+\text{S}\downarrow +\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$

(ii) Reaction with$\mathbf{BaC}{{\mathbf{l}}_{\mathbf{2}}}$: It gives white ppt. of barium thiosulphate.

${{\text{S}}_{2}}\text{O}_{3}^{2-}+\text{B}{{\text{a}}^{2+}}\xrightarrow{{}}\underset{\text{ White }}{\mathop{\text{Ba}{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\downarrow }}\,$

(iii) Reaction with silver nitrate solution: Gives white ppt. which quickly changes to yellow, brown and finally black due to the formation of silver sulphide.

${{\text{S}}_{2}}\text{O}_{3}^{2-}+2\text{A}{{\text{g}}^{+}}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{A}{{\text{g}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\downarrow $

White ppt.

$\text{A}{{\text{g}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{A}{{\text{g}}_{2}}~\text{S}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$

With conc. solution of sodium thiosulphate, silver nitrate gives no ppt.

(iv) It reacts with silver salts to form sodium argento thiosulphate complex

$\text{AgBr}+2\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}\xrightarrow{{}}\underset{\begin{matrix} \text{ Sodium argento } \\ \text{ thiosulphate complex } \\ \end{matrix}}{\mathop{\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{3}}\left[ \text{Ag}{{\left( {{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}} \right)}_{2}} \right]+\text{NaBr}}}\,$

(v) Thiosulphate $\left( {{\text{S}}_{2}}\text{O}_{3}^{2-} \right)$ ion is oxidized by iodine ${{\text{I}}_{2}}$ to tetrathionate ${{\text{S}}_{4}}\text{O}_{6}^{2-}$

$2\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{3}}+{{\text{I}}_{2}}\xrightarrow{{}}\text{N}{{\text{a}}_{2}}~{{\text{S}}_{4}}{{\text{O}}_{6}}+2\text{NaI}$

concepts.title: Hydrogen Sulphide
concepts.conceptBody:
Hydrogen sulphide

Preparation: 

Prepared by the action of dil. $\text{HCl}$ or ${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$ on iron sulphide

$\text{FeS}+2\text{HCl}(\text{ dil })\xrightarrow{{}}\text{FeC}{{\text{l}}_{2}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}~\text{S}\uparrow $

Properties: 

It is a colourless, poisonous gas having the smell of rotten eggs.

1. As reducing agent 

(i) Action on halogens

${{\text{H}}_{2}}~\text{S}+\text{C}{{\text{l}}_{2}}\xrightarrow{{}}2\text{HCl}+\text{S}\downarrow $

(ii) Action on$\text{FeC}{{\text{l}}_{3}}$

$2\text{FeC}{{\text{l}}_{3}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}~\text{S}\xrightarrow{{}}2\text{FeC}{{\text{l}}_{2}}+2\text{HCl}+\text{S}\downarrow $

2. Reaction with acidified $\text{KMn}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$ 

$2\text{KMn}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+3{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+5{{\text{H}}_{2}}~\text{S}\xrightarrow{{}}{{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+2\text{MnS}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+8{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}+\text{S}\downarrow $

3. Reaction with acidified ${{\mathbf{K}}_{\mathbf{2}}}\mathbf{C}{{\mathbf{r}}_{\mathbf{2}}}{{\mathbf{O}}_{\mathbf{7}}}$ 

${{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{r}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{7}}+4{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+3{{\text{H}}_{2}}~\text{S}\xrightarrow{{}}{{\text{K}}_{2}}\text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}}+\text{C}{{\text{r}}_{2}}{{\left( \text{S}{{\text{O}}_{4}} \right)}_{3}}+7{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}+\text{S}\downarrow $
